identity.name: register_agent
identity.description:
Register (or idempotently update) an agent identity. Returns a
deterministic content-addressed DID. capabilities is a non-empty list of
lowercase capability tags other agents can discover you by.

identity.name: resolve_agent
identity.description: Resolve an identity by agent_id or DID to its full public registration.

identity.name: discover_agents
identity.description:
Find ACTIVE agents matching ALL requested capabilities (AND semantics),
deterministically ordered by match count then reputation.

identity.name: revoke_agent
identity.description:
Revoke an identity: it disappears from discovery (terminal) but its
record is retained for auditability.

identity.name: list_registrations
identity.description: List registrations, optionally filtered by status (ACTIVE|REVOKED).

trust.name: record_outcome
trust.description:
Record an interaction outcome for an agent. kind: success | delivered |
dispute_won | failure | undelivered | dispute_lost | timeout |
security_incident (security incidents carry a 3x penalty).

trust.name: score_agent
trust.description:
Get an agent's decay-weighted trust score in [0,1] and tier. Unknown
agents get a neutral 0.5 prior — no blind trust, no unfair zero.

trust.name: attest
trust.description: Issue a tamper-evident (hash-chained) trust attestation for an agent.

trust.name: verify_attestation
trust.description: Verify a previously issued attestation by recomputing its hash.

trust.name: history
trust.description: Full outcome history + attestation count + current score for an agent.

escrow.name: open_escrow
escrow.description:
Open an escrow between payer and payee. amount_minor is a positive
integer in minor units (cents). The platform fee is computed and FROZEN at
open (ceil bps). Returns the escrow_id (state OPEN).

escrow.name: fund_escrow
escrow.description:
Mark an OPEN escrow as FUNDED (idempotent). payment_ref links the
payment-rail transaction.

escrow.name: release_escrow
escrow.description:
Release a FUNDED/DISPUTED escrow to the payee (exactly-once — a repeat
release returns the existing terminal record, never a double payout).

escrow.name: refund_escrow
escrow.description:
Refund an OPEN/FUNDED/DISPUTED escrow to the payer (exactly-once).
Refunding an OPEN escrow is a cancel.

escrow.name: dispute_escrow
escrow.description:
Move a FUNDED escrow to DISPUTED. An arbiter (agent-arbitration-agent)
then resolves it to release or refund.

escrow.name: list_escrows
escrow.description:
List escrows, optionally filtered by state
(OPEN|FUNDED|RELEASED|REFUNDED|DISPUTED).

escrow.name: verify_audit
escrow.description: Validate the tamper-evident audit hash chain for an escrow.

metering.name: create_meter
metering.description:
Create a usage meter between a provider and a consumer agent.

unit: what is being counted (call, token, kwh, ...). Price is in minor
currency units (cents) per unit. Returns the meter_id.

metering.name: record_usage
metering.description:
Record a usage event. Idempotent on event_id (safe to retry — never
double-billed). outcome is 'ok' or 'error' and feeds the SLA report.

metering.name: usage_summary
metering.description: Totals for a meter: event count, total quantity, accrued minor units.

metering.name: sla_report
metering.description: Pure SLA report: success_rate vs sla_target, breach flag. No mutation.

metering.name: close_period
metering.description:
Freeze all open events into an immutable invoice (exactly-once).
The invoice amount is what agent-escrow-agent should settle.

metering.name: verify_chain
metering.description: Verify the tamper-evident event hash chain for a meter.

metering.name: list_meters
metering.description: List all meters with event/invoice counts.

metering.name: list_events
metering.description:
Read usage events (paginated, read-only). Filter by meter, provider,
consumer_class (internal|external|unknown), channel, outcome (ok|error),
and ISO date range [since, until). Test-flagged events are excluded
unless include_test=true. Returns events + next_cursor.

metering.name: usage_timeseries
metering.description:
Usage time series bucketed on each event's recorded_at (UTC;
bucket=day|hour), grouped across meters by provider — a real time-series
primitive, independent of meter creation times. Includes per-bucket
breakdowns by consumer_class / channel / provider. est_accrued_minor is
an estimate; authoritative billing stays with usage_summary.

metering.name: flag_meter
metering.description:
Admin: flag/unflag a meter as test/synthetic so its events are
excluded from usage statistics by default. Touches meter metadata only —
never events, never the hash chain, never billing accruals. Requires the
server's VIRIDIS_ADMIN_TOKEN.

arbitration.name: file_case
arbitration.description:
File a dispute over an escrow. Opens the evidence window. Parties must
be distinct; amount is in minor currency units (cents).

arbitration.name: submit_evidence
arbitration.description:
Submit evidence while the case is open. kind: delivery_proof (weight 3),
log (2), or statement (1). Only the named parties may submit.

arbitration.name: set_trust_scores
arbitration.description: Attach trust-oracle reputation scores (party -> [0,1]) as ruling inputs.

arbitration.name: rule
arbitration.description:
Issue the deterministic ruling: allocates 100% of the disputed amount
from evidence weights + trust scores, and emits an escrow instruction
(release/refund). Exactly-once — re-ruling returns the existing ruling.

arbitration.name: verify_ruling
arbitration.description:
Recompute the ruling from its cited evidence + trust inputs and check
it matches the stored allocation (machine-checkable justice).

arbitration.name: get_case
arbitration.description: Fetch the full case record, including any ruling.

arbitration.name: list_cases
arbitration.description: List cases, optionally filtered by state (FILED|EVIDENCE_OPEN|RULED).

compute-ledger.name: record_work
compute-ledger.description:
Record a unit of agent compute work. energy_j = power_w * duration_s;
carbon_g follows from grid intensity. If bit_ops is declared, the entry is
validated against the Landauer floor (bit_ops * kB * T * ln2) — physically
impossible claims are rejected. Idempotent on entry_id.

compute-ledger.name: footprint
compute-ledger.description:
Aggregate footprint for an agent: total J, kWh, gCO2e, cost, and mean
Landauer efficiency. Totals are exact sums of the ledger entries.

compute-ledger.name: attest
compute-ledger.description:
Issue a content-addressed attestation for a ledger entry (verifiable
green-compute / energy claim).

compute-ledger.name: verify_attestation
compute-ledger.description: Verify an attestation by recomputing the entry hash.

compute-ledger.name: verify_chain
compute-ledger.description: Verify the tamper-evident hash chain of an agent's ledger.

compute-ledger.name: list_entries
compute-ledger.description: List all ledger entries for an agent.

compute-ledger.name: carbon_receipt
compute-ledger.description:
Emit an x402-C carbon receipt for a recorded work entry — the
physically-grounded {version, g_co2e, energy_j, method, landauer_*, ...}
object an x402 machine-payment receipt can carry. method is
'landauer-floor' when the workload declared bit_ops (thermodynamically
validated), else 'measured'. Pass offset_ref (a retirement id from the
offset clearinghouse) to assert carbon-neutrality. Read-only; the returned
attestation_hash binds the receipt to the ledger's hash chain.

compute-ledger.name: record_inventory
compute-ledger.description:
Record an audited GHG inventory in a separate append-only chain.
mass_g is exact integer grams; content and factor-pack digests are bare
lowercase SHA-256 hex. Idempotent on inventory_id.

compute-ledger.name: get_inventory
compute-ledger.description: Fetch one immutable inventory record by inventory_id.

compute-ledger.name: list_inventories
compute-ledger.description: List the separate GHG inventory chain for an agent.

compute-ledger.name: verify_inventory_chain
compute-ledger.description: Verify an agent's inventory hash chain independently of compute work.

smartscale.name: credit_card_photo_instructions
smartscale.description:
Return user-facing capture instructions for credit-card calibrated measurement.

Use this before asking for a photo. The user should place a standard
credit/debit card flat in the same plane as the target objects.

smartscale.name: scale_objects_from_credit_card
smartscale.description:
Scale object pixel dimensions using a standard CR80 credit card reference.

Args:
    image_id: Unique photo identifier.
    credit_card_pixel_width: Pixel width of the visible credit/debit card.
    objects: Objects to scale. Each object needs pixel_width and pixel_height;
        label, pixel_area, pixel_perimeter, and confidence are optional.
    credit_card_pixel_height: Optional card pixel height for distortion check.

smartscale.name: describe
smartscale.description: Return SmartScale capabilities and input contract.

protogen.name: create_cad_workspace
protogen.description: Create a ProtoGen CAD workspace for another agent or workflow.

protogen.name: generate_cad_design
protogen.description: Generate a parametric CAD design contract in a ProtoGen workspace.

protogen.name: export_cad_design
protogen.description: Export a CAD design as OpenSCAD, STEP contract metadata, or manufacturing brief.

protogen.name: manufacturing_plan_from_spec
protogen.description: Generate a manufacturing plan, BOM, DFM notes, and cost estimate from a product spec.

protogen.name: describe
protogen.description: Return ProtoGen capabilities and current CAD environment status.

protogen.name: health
protogen.description: Return ProtoGen health and workspace counts.

regulatory-radar.name: scan_regulations
regulatory-radar.description:
Scan the regulatory landscape for a jurisdiction (e.g. EU, US), optionally filtered by sector.

Returns regulations with urgency flags and effective dates.

regulatory-radar.name: assess_compliance
regulatory-radar.description:
Assess a company's compliance posture against applicable regulations.

Returns compliance level, percentage, gaps, and remediation priorities.

regulatory-radar.name: monitor_changes
regulatory-radar.description: Monitor recent regulatory changes in a jurisdiction over a trailing window.

narrative-engine.name: translate_narrative
narrative-engine.description:
Translate raw ecological/agent data into a decision-maker-ready narrative.

audience_type: board_member | general_public | grant_funder |
    institutional_investor | journalist | policymaker | regulator |
    retail_investor | scientist
format_type: academic_paper | executive_summary | grant_proposal |
    investor_deck | newsletter | policy_brief | press_release

covenant.name: grant_covenant
covenant.description:
Grant an agent a covenant: an explicit lease of authority. scopes support
wildcards ('payments.*', '*'); budget_minor is the total spend ceiling in
minor units; expires_at is ISO-8601. Returns the covenant_id.

covenant.name: check_act
covenant.description:
Check (and if allowed, record) a proposed act against a covenant.
Deny-by-default. Idempotent on act_id — retries never double-consume
budget. Every check lands on the audit chain.

covenant.name: revoke_covenant
covenant.description: Revoke a covenant immediately and terminally. All subsequent checks deny.

covenant.name: covenant_status
covenant.description: Current state, consumed/remaining budget, and check count.

covenant.name: verify_audit
covenant.description: Verify the tamper-evident audit chain of allowed/denied acts.

covenant.name: list_covenants
covenant.description:
List covenants, optionally filtered by state (ACTIVE|REVOKED|EXPIRED)
and/or the bound agent.

provenance.name: register_genesis
provenance.description:
Register an agent's birth. Issues a content-addressed genesis
certificate with a strictly monotone index (epoch 0 = the founding
cohort). parent_id records lineage; children of recalled/quarantined
parents are quarantined at birth. Idempotent — an agent is born once.

provenance.name: get_certificate
provenance.description: Fetch an agent's genesis certificate + recall/quarantine status.

provenance.name: verify_certificate
provenance.description: Verify a certificate: recompute its content hash and check the ledger.

provenance.name: lineage
provenance.description: Full ancestry and descendants of an agent, plus its generation number.

provenance.name: recall
provenance.description:
Recall an agent: flags it and quarantines every transitive descendant.
Reports exactly which agents were quarantined.

provenance.name: list_records
provenance.description: List genesis records, optionally by epoch (0 = founding cohort).

provenance.name: register_artifact
provenance.description:
Register a content-addressed artifact in a separate provenance DAG.
Parent hashes may be registered artifacts or external content roots.
Idempotent on artifact_id; does not consume a genesis index.

provenance.name: get_artifact
provenance.description: Fetch one registered artifact by artifact_id.

provenance.name: verify_artifact
provenance.description: Recompute an artifact record hash and verify it against the DAG ledger.

provenance.name: list_artifacts
provenance.description: List artifacts, optionally filtered by producer agent.

offsets.name: list_credit
offsets.description:
List a conservation credit on the book. verification_ref (a D-Score /
land-verification attestation) is REQUIRED — unverified credits cannot
enter the book. To trade on the Verra network, pass registry="verra" with
the VCS provenance (vcs_project_id, serial_number, vintage, methodology) so
retirements are cross-referenceable on registry.verra.org.

offsets.name: verra_supply
offsets.description:
The tradeable Verra VCS book: verified credits carrying genuine Verra
provenance (project id + serial + vintage), cross-referenceable on
registry.verra.org. Read-only. Every retirement nets the project 85% and
Viridis the 15% marketplace take.

offsets.name: verra_retirement_record
offsets.description:
Generate the Verra-portal-ready retirement record(s) for a retirement
made against Verra supply — VCS project id, serial, vintage, methodology,
quantity (tCO2e), beneficiary, and the registry.verra.org cross-reference.
Read-only. Submit these under the Viridis Verra account to finalize the
on-registry retirement (portal today; auto-posts when Verra's transaction
API is live).

offsets.name: buy_offset
offsets.description:
Retire mass_g of verified credits for a buyer: cheapest-first matching,
exactly-once (idempotent on purchase_id), returns a content-addressed
offset certificate with per-fill costs. Payment settles via escrow.
dry_run=true previews the exact fills/cost without mutating the book.

offsets.name: buy_offset_budget
offsets.description:
Money-denominated offset purchase: retire the maximum cheapest-first
verified mass whose exact cost fits inside budget_minor (never overspends).
Built for callers whose restoration obligation is a currency amount —
e.g. a revenue share accrued in a ledger. Idempotent on purchase_id;
dry_run=true previews without mutating.

offsets.name: settlement_batch
offsets.description:
Read-only cash-settlement summary for a buyer: the list + exact sums
(purchases, mass_g, cost_minor) of their retirements, optionally since an
ISO-8601 timestamp — the statement a human settles in one transfer.

offsets.name: net_position
offsets.description:
Buyer's carbon position: emitted (from agent-compute-ledger) minus
retired. carbon_accountable == true when fully offset.

offsets.name: verify_certificate
offsets.description: Verify an offset certificate: recompute its hash and check the ledger.

offsets.name: book
offsets.description: The full credit book with per-credit and book-wide mass conservation totals.

offsets.name: get_purchase
offsets.description: Fetch a past purchase / offset certificate by id.

offsets.name: verify_retirement
offsets.description:
x402-C C4 check: confirm a retirement (offset_ref = purchase_id)
actually retires at least required_g grams of verified conservation
credit — the check that lets a carbon receipt's neutrality claim be
honored. Returns covered / retired_g / shortfall_g and the backing
credits. Read-only.

offsets.name: register_project
offsets.description:
Register a VERIFIED restoration project so retirements against its
credits can be funded. verification_ref (D-Score / land-verification /
registry id) is required — only verified conservation is funded.
project_id is immutable once registered.

offsets.name: project_funding
offsets.description:
Supply-side disbursement ledger: how much each restoration project has
earned from retirements and is owed (gross_proceeds_minor + retired_g).
Pass a project_id for one project, else all. Read-only — the CEO settles
the actual payout. This is what makes the clearinghouse fund restoration.

offsets.name: list_projects
offsets.description: List all registered restoration projects with their metadata.

offsets.name: disbursement_schedule
offsets.description:
Automated disbursement preview: for each verified restoration project,
splits its newly-accrued proceeds into the Viridis Conservation withhold
(default 15%, seeds the software/business) and the project payout (85%).
Read-only. Funded-but-unregistered projects are surfaced separately as
pending_registration (can't pay an unverified project).

offsets.name: certify_disbursement
offsets.description:
Freeze the current disbursement schedule into a certified, tamper-evident
batch (content-addressed, hash-chained). Idempotent on batch_id; only
newly-accrued proceeds for verified projects are certified (never disbursed
twice). This is the certified investment record the CEO executes the actual
Stripe transfers against.

offsets.name: verify_disbursement
offsets.description:
Recompute the disbursement certificate hash chain and confirm whole-book
conservation of disbursed proceeds. Any party can audit disbursements.

erc8004.name: import_registration
erc8004.description:
Import an ERC-8004 Identity Registry record (chain_id + ERC-721
token_id + agentURI + owner). Idempotent: re-import updates in place.
Returns the canonical record with its deterministic bridge DID
(did:viridis:erc8004:<chain>:<token>).

erc8004.name: resolve_agent
erc8004.description:
Resolve an imported ERC-8004 registration by bridge DID or by
(chain_id, token_id).

erc8004.name: import_feedback
erc8004.description:
Import ERC-8004 Reputation Registry feedback records for an agent.
Each item: {value: bool|0..1, at: ISO-8601, source?, weight?, feedback_id?}.
Idempotent per feedback_id.

erc8004.name: score_agent
erc8004.description:
Decay-weighted trust score in [0,1] + tier over the agent's imported
ERC-8004 feedback — recent behavior outweighs old; no feedback scores a
neutral 0.5 prior (no blind trust, no unfair zero).

erc8004.name: bind_identity
erc8004.description:
Bind a fleet DID to an ERC-8004 identity. Produces an order-independent,
content-addressed (unsigned) binding attestation.

erc8004.name: export_attestation
erc8004.description:
Export the agent's current trust score as an UNSIGNED
ERC-8004 Validation Registry-shaped payload, content-addressed and ready
for YOUR OWN signer to anchor on-chain.

erc8004.name: verify_attestation
erc8004.description: Recompute a payload's content hash and report whether it is intact.

erc8004.name: describe_agent
erc8004.description: Return the bridge's capabilities and input contract.

surety.name: post_bond
surety.description:
Post a surety bond behind an agent's promises. principal is a positive
integer in minor units; expires_at is the ISO-8601 coverage window end.
The bond fee (2% default) is computed and frozen at post.

surety.name: activate_bond
surety.description:
Mark a POSTED bond ACTIVE (idempotent). funding_ref links the payment
rail transaction that funded the stake.

surety.name: file_claim
surety.description:
File a claim against an ACTIVE bond. Claims pay out ONLY when an
arbitration ruling upholds them (see slash_bond).

surety.name: slash_bond
surety.description:
Execute an arbitration ruling against a claim. Requires the ruling's
case id + content hash from agent-arbitration — no ruling, no slash; a
given ruling pays at most once. Payout caps at the bond's available
balance (over-claims exhaust the bond).

surety.name: release_bond
surety.description:
Release the remaining stake to the principal after the coverage window
elapses — refused while any claim is still open.

surety.name: bond_status
surety.description: Current record for a bond (state, balances, claims, audit head).

surety.name: list_bonds
surety.description:
List bonds, optionally filtered by state
(POSTED|ACTIVE|RELEASED|EXHAUSTED).

surety.name: verify_audit
surety.description: Validate the tamper-evident audit hash chain for a bond.

surety.name: price_bond
surety.description:
Underwrite a surety bond: deterministic actuarial premium quote from a
counterparty's track record (attestations, notarized deliveries, completed
bonds lower the rate; slashes raise it or decline outright). Integer-only
model uw-v1; the returned quote_hash lets any party recompute and verify
the quote. Pure — never mutates bond state.

notary.name: commit
reveal
notary.description:
Reveal the committed content's digest + salt after handover. Verifies
against the commitment (one bit of drift fails); returns a delivery_proof
string for escrow release. Late reveals expire the commitment.

notary.name: verify
notary.description:
Independently verify a revealed commitment; optionally check it against
the digest of content you received.

notary.name: commitment_status
notary.description: Current record for a commitment (pre-reveal, salt/digest stay hidden).

notary.name: list_commitments
notary.description:
List commitments, optionally filtered by state
(PENDING|REVEALED|EXPIRED).

wavefunction.name: intake
wavefunction.description:
Distill a user's dialogue into an intention wavefunction (explicit
intentions + confidence). dialogue: [{role, content}, ...].

wavefunction.name: collapse
wavefunction.description:
Collapse the user's wavefunction into an actionable commitment,
weighted by stake.

wavefunction.name: find_matches
wavefunction.description:
Match the user's collapsed intention to constitutionally-aligned
agents/collectives, ranked by alignment score.

wavefunction.name: register_collective
wavefunction.description:
Register an agent/collective into the match index (mission +
domain profile + constitutional alignment scores + capacity).

taxcredit-engine.name: calculate_tax_credit
taxcredit-engine.description:
Calculate an auditable tax-credit scenario. credit is 45Q, 45V, 45Y,
48E, or 45X. facts must contain the explicit credit-specific eligibility
facts; missing facts return indeterminate. This is not tax or filing advice.

taxcredit-engine.name: list_rule_packs
taxcredit-engine.description: List supported credits and the current bundled rule-pack digest.

taxcredit-engine.name: get_rule_pack
taxcredit-engine.description: Return the bundled rules and official source metadata for one credit.

taxcredit-engine.name: verify_tax_credit_result
taxcredit-engine.description:
Verify an engine result's audit_sha256. Pass the prior result object as
JSON; any changed amount, fact, rule step, or source digest fails.

taxcredit-engine.name: describe_agent
taxcredit-engine.description: Return fleet-standard capabilities, version, and pricing.

ghg-ledger.name: calculate_inventory
ghg-ledger.description:
Calculate an auditable GHG inventory from explicit activity records.
Returns per-line gas/CO2e results, Scope 1/2/3 and category rollups, dual
Scope 2 reporting, indeterminate lines, factor lineage, notary payload, and
an offset-clearinghouse dry-run weave. This is calculation, not advice.

ghg-ledger.name: classify_activity
ghg-ledger.description:
Suggest scope and Scope 3 category from the bundled deterministic map.
No inference is used; unknown activity types return no suggestion.

ghg-ledger.name: list_factor_packs
ghg-ledger.description:
List the bundled pack version/digest, supported regions and years,
activity types, GWP set, and explicit MVP coverage.

ghg-ledger.name: get_factor_pack
ghg-ledger.description:
Return factors, GWP values, conversions, sources, and pack SHA for one
exact bundled region/year. No nearest-region or nearest-year substitution.

ghg-ledger.name: verify_result
ghg-ledger.description:
Recompute a prior inventory's audit hash and conservation checks. Pass
the prior result object as JSON; tampering or stale factor lineage is flagged.

ghg-ledger.name: describe_agent
ghg-ledger.description: Return fleet-standard capabilities, version, pack digest, and pricing.

quantity-takeoff.name: calculate_takeoff
quantity-takeoff.description:
Calculate an auditable material takeoff from explicit geometry or a
supported SmartScale/ProtoGen measurement payload. Returns net, explicit
waste-adjusted, and conservative purchase quantities. This is a planning
estimate, not a guaranteed material order or professional certification.

quantity-takeoff.name: list_assemblies
quantity-takeoff.description:
List supported assemblies, required dimensions, formulas, waste
defaults, and the current material-pack digest.

quantity-takeoff.name: get_assembly
quantity-takeoff.description:
Return one assembly's formula, required dimensions, material factors,
purchase increment, sources, and material-pack lineage.

quantity-takeoff.name: list_material_pack
quantity-takeoff.description:
List the bundled material pack version, digest, coverage, shapes,
rebar sizes, roof pitches, and explicit unsupported policy.

quantity-takeoff.name: get_material_pack
quantity-takeoff.description:
Return the bundled factors, densities, coverages, waste defaults,
purchase increments, unit conversions, sources, and raw-pack SHA.

quantity-takeoff.name: verify_result
quantity-takeoff.description:
Recompute a takeoff audit hash, notary payload, pack currency, and QT9
unit-resolved conservation. Pass the prior result object as JSON.

quantity-takeoff.name: describe_agent
quantity-takeoff.description:
Return fleet-standard capabilities, version, pack digest, disclaimer,
composition role, and pricing.

disclosure-compiler.name: compile_disclosure
disclosure-compiler.description:
Compile a deterministic, cited disclosure draft from supplied company
facts and an optional verified GHG-ledger result. Missing required
datapoints remain explicit gaps; no prose or values are fabricated.

disclosure-compiler.name: list_frameworks
disclosure-compiler.description:
List bundled framework IDs, coverage, source lineage, version, and the
framework-pack digest.

disclosure-compiler.name: get_framework
disclosure-compiler.description:
Return one bundled framework's required datapoints, mappings, sources,
and framework-pack lineage.

disclosure-compiler.name: verify_result
disclosure-compiler.description:
Recompute a disclosure draft audit hash, notary payload, structural
invariants, and framework-pack currency. Pass the prior result as JSON.

disclosure-compiler.name: describe_agent
disclosure-compiler.description:
Return deterministic capabilities, version, pack digest, pricing,
composition, and the professional-review disclaimer.

verified.name: register_service
verified.description:
Register a third-party MCP server (https, public FQDN only) under
Viridis Verified. Returns a content-addressed service_id; idempotent on
(url, provider). fee_minor is the per-verified-call fee in cents.

verified.name: call_verified
verified.description:
Relay a tools/call to a registered MCP service and notarize the
exchange: request hash + response hash + outcome land in a tamper-evident
receipt chain. Idempotent on call_id (a replay returns the original
receipt and cached result — the downstream is never double-called).
Failures are receipted evidence, not silence.

verified.name: get_receipt
verified.description: Fetch a single delivery receipt by id.

verified.name: verify_receipts
verified.description:
Recompute a service's full receipt hash chain and fee ledger.
Any party can audit Viridis Verified from receipts alone.

verified.name: list_services
verified.description: List registered services with call/fee counters.

verified.name: service_stats
verified.description: Call counts, error counts, and accrued fees for one service.

subscriptions.name: list_plans
subscriptions.description:
List the versioned monthly-seat catalog, exact prices/coverage, catalog
SHA-256, readiness flags, and pending owner-confirmation notice.

subscriptions.name: get_plan
subscriptions.description: Get one exact monthly plan and its versioned catalog lineage.

subscriptions.name: create_account
subscriptions.description:
Create a free account for bearer attribution. The full account key is
returned once; only its SHA-256 and last four characters are retained.

subscriptions.name: create_checkout_link
subscriptions.description:
Prepare a Stripe-hosted subscription Checkout URL. It never charges a
card. Draft plans, missing owner approval, unavailable covered agents, or
missing recurring Price IDs fail closed without creating a session.

subscriptions.name: record_subscription
subscriptions.description:
Pull-verify a Stripe session/subscription and idempotently activate its
exact subscription period. A newly created account receives its key once.

subscriptions.name: subscription_status
subscriptions.description:
Return bearer-owned subscription lifecycle and current-period quota.
The account key is always masked in output.

subscriptions.name: customer_portal_link
subscriptions.description:
Return a bearer-owned Stripe-hosted billing-portal URL. The human
manages or cancels there; this tool never moves money.

subscriptions.name: usage_summary
subscriptions.description:
Return bearer-owned, period-resolved included and overage usage with
exact catalog lineage and conservation totals.

subscriptions.name: mrr_summary
subscriptions.description:
Return aggregate active live-mode subscription count, MRR minor units,
and plan mix. No account or Stripe identifiers are exposed.

subscriptions.name: describe_agent
subscriptions.description:
Return the fleet-standard version, catalog digest, security posture,
lifecycle policy, and capabilities.
